pattern: A 90-degree clockwise rotation in place is a transpose followed by reversing each row, each step touching only swaps so no second matrix is allocated.
hints: The element at (row, col) lands at (col, n-1-row) after a clockwise quarter turn.
Transposing across the main diagonal then mirroring each row left-to-right composes into exactly that mapping.
First swap matrix[i][j] with matrix[j][i] for i<j (transpose), then reverse every row in place; the matrix argument is mutated and nothing is returned.

edge_inputs.description: negative values must move with their cells
all-equal matrix is unchanged but must still be valid

stress.description: random square matrix sized within the constraint to exercise the transpose-and-reverse pipeline
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(20, size))
    n = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    return [[[rng.randrange(-1000, 1001) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]]
stress.note: varied n up to the cap covers odd and even side lengths
